opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I woke up covered in little red dots everywhere. I had a sore throat last week and took antibiotics, and now I'm worried I'm having an allergic reaction.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The rash came up fast tonight and I panicked it was a drug allergy or something serious.

symptom_narrative.character: lots of small red teardrop-shaped spots with fine scale
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: mildly itchy; no lip/tongue swelling, no wheeze or breathlessness, no fever

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: strep throat last week, finished penicillin 3 days ago

family_history_as_reported.narrative: a parent has an autoimmune skin condition

objective_data_offered.value: hundreds of small teardrop-shaped red spots with fine silvery scale on trunk and back; when I press a glass against them they fade (blanch); no swelling of lips/face
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-performed glass test on video; reassuring but not a formal dermatological examination
